tickets.title: No /api/llm-call endpoint exists
tickets.summary: The route table has no /api/llm-call. Without it the LLM-collaborator loop has nowhere to POST a prompt. Cross-cluster dupe with settings-and-providers — flagged here from the API-surface angle.
tickets.evidence: Grep across the switch in _handleApiRequest() returns zero matches for llm-call.
tickets.proposed: Add a POST handler that takes {provider, model, messages, settingsCategory?}, looks up provider credentials via persistence.getSettingsByCategory(), and proxies.
tickets.commitHash: a29616f (Wave 5E settings cluster close — POST /api/llm-call backend route shipped there)
tickets.actionsTaken: Resolved upstream by Wave 5E settings cluster — see docs/_pending-tickets/settings-and-providers.for-review.json ticket 1 (verdict: ACK by wave-5e-reviewer). The POST /api/llm-call route is implemented at src/core/server/app.js:2317 (_handleLlmCall) with X-St8-Secret auth, 8KB body cap (413 if exceeded), entryId+prompt validation (400), getSettingsByCategory lookup (404 if entry missing), dispatch via src/features/llm/dispatcher.js to anthropic + openai adapters (501 stubs for google/ollama/lmstudio/openrouter/custom). 8 route tests in tests/core/server/llm-call-route.test.js including the decrypt-routing probe (raw DB row contains ciphertext, adapter receives plaintext). No re-implementation needed in Wave 5F.

tickets.title: Ticket lifecycle endpoints (claim / resolve) are missing
tickets.summary: Only POST / GET on /api/tickets and GET /api/tickets/count are exposed. St8Persistence has the lifecycle primitives (createTicket, getOpenTickets, countOpenTickets) but no claim() / resolve() HTTP wrappers. The LLM-collaborator loop cannot close the workflow over HTTP.
tickets.evidence: Switch only handles '/api/tickets' and '/api/tickets/count' — no regex match for '/api/tickets/<id>/...'.
tickets.proposed: Add POST /api/tickets/:id/claim and POST /api/tickets/:id/resolve. Fire HOOKS.TICKET_CLAIMED / TICKET_RESOLVED.

tickets.title: /api/connection-state.json reads file, not SQLite — can lag a pass
tickets.summary: _serveManifest reads the on-disk connection-state.json. main.js writes the manifest as part of the INDEX_COMPLETE subscriber chain, AFTER all SQLite upserts are committed. During the gap between SQLite commit and manifest write, /api/connection-state.json returns stale data while /api/needs-ai-review (DB-backed) returns fresh data — inconsistent.
tickets.proposed: Either (a) generate the manifest JSON on demand from SQLite, or (b) document the staleness contract and add an `?fresh=1` query flag that triggers a regeneration.

tickets.title: /api/exec referenced by terminal.js but absent from route table
tickets.summary: terminal.js phreakExecute falls back to POST /api/exec when the EPO client is disconnected. /api/exec is not in app.js's switch — the request 404s.
tickets.proposed: Either implement /api/exec (carefully sandboxed) or remove the dead fallback path. Same for the PHREAK_API constant on line 43 ('/api/v1/exec' — already commented as dead).

tickets.title: graph-viewer.js loads D3 from a CDN
tickets.summary: loadD3() injects <script src='https://d3js.org/d3.v7.min.js'>. This breaks the desktop-first / offline stance the founder set during Sonic integration. Also a CSP-shape liability.
tickets.proposed: Bundle D3 locally OR replace the connection graph with a particles/Three.js node-graph view (see roadmap P2).

tickets.title: terminal.js depends on window.renderFileList — global is undefined
tickets.summary: _isolateFiles() calls window.renderFileList(), and _clearVoid() targets #void-file-list. Neither exists in the current frontend tree — these were part of the founder's pre-session 'void file list' pop-out which never landed in the carousel layout. The terminal silently no-ops.
tickets.proposed: Either (a) delete the dead branches (isolate-RED, isolate-YELLOW, isolate-GREEN, ALL, clear-void), or (b) implement #void-file-list as a third carousel slide and wire renderFileList into a shared service.

tickets.title: file-explorer.js error-banner hoist uses MutationObserver — fragile
tickets.summary: The shell uses a MutationObserver to detect when .explorer-error-banner appears inside the explorer host, then forcibly moves it into the column titlebar. Any change to where the banner renders inside file-explorer.js (e.g. wrapping in a new div) breaks the presentation silently.
tickets.proposed: Either have file-explorer.js emit a CustomEvent the shell listens for, or expose a slot/portal in the explorer's render contract.

tickets.title: file-explorer.js _toggleHidden references undefined LS_SHOW_HIDDEN
tickets.summary: _toggleHidden() calls localStorage.setItem(LS_SHOW_HIDDEN, ...) but LS_SHOW_HIDDEN is never declared. The function is also never wired into the rendered UI (no button calls it).
tickets.proposed: Remove _toggleHidden and the showHidden state, or define LS_SHOW_HIDDEN and add a toggle button.

tickets.title: graph-viewer modal has no Escape handler and no focus trap
tickets.summary: showGraphPopup() appends a .graph-popup-overlay to document.body; close is only the diamond button. No Escape key close, no focus trap, no aria-modal — inconsistent with .panel-overlay (used by PRD wizard) which sets role='dialog' aria-modal='true'.
tickets.proposed: Migrate showGraphPopup to the same .panel-overlay pattern used by the PRD wizard, or add an Escape handler + aria attributes.

tickets.title: PRD wizard kept as modal — not migrated to slide-carousel
tickets.summary: The PRD wizard remains a modal panel-overlay rather than a fourth carousel slide. Frontend agent explicitly kept it — re-flagging from the API/legacy angle because file-explorer.js:362 hard-codes onclick='window.openPRDWizard()' which assumes the modal pattern stays.
tickets.proposed: Decide: keep as modal (and document why) or migrate to a 4th slide. Either way file-explorer.js shouldn't need to know which it is.

tickets.title: /api/tickets and /api/record-commit have no body-size limit
tickets.summary: 17 POST handlers cap the body at 1KB; two others cap at 2KB. /api/record-commit and /api/tickets accept arbitrarily large bodies — they read `req.on('data', chunk => body += chunk)` with no MAX_BODY_SIZE guard. The pattern was added later and skipped the convention.
tickets.proposed: Backfill the MAX_BODY_SIZE check (4KB is reasonable for a commit payload with filesChanged; 8KB for tickets with identityBundle).

tickets.title: /api/files traversal protection uses path.relative — verify every other route
tickets.summary: _handleFileList correctly uses path.relative() to enforce path boundaries. The earlier startsWith()-based check was insecure. We should audit every other route that takes a path-shaped input — _handleIndex (`{path}`), _handleVerify (`{path}`), _handleConceptFile (`{filepath}`), _handleMarkReviewed (`{filepath}`), and _serveStaticFile (line 174 uses startsWith() — same insecure pattern that was fixed elsewhere).
tickets.proposed: Replace startsWith() checks with path.relative() in _serveStaticFile and audit all path-accepting handlers for traversal.

tickets.title: Body parsing is duplicated across ~17 handlers
tickets.summary: Every POST handler reimplements the same data/end/close/413 dance with subtle drift (record-commit and tickets omit the cap; some catch close-event leaks, others don't — only _handleSettings has a close handler that closes the persistence; the rest can leak DB connections on client abort).
tickets.proposed: Extract a readJsonBody(req, res, {maxSize}) helper that returns a Promise<object|null>. Wrap each handler.

tickets.title: No OpenAPI / Swagger document for the route table
tickets.summary: There is no machine-readable description of the 23 routes. The inventory in this cluster doc is hand-authored. New consumers (Sonic, an LLM collaborator) have no way to introspect the surface.
tickets.proposed: Either generate openapi.json from a route descriptor (refactor the switch into a table-driven registry) or check in a manual openapi.yaml and add a /api/openapi.json route.

tickets.title: REST conventions are mixed
tickets.summary: /api/prd-projects/<name> uses path params via regex (line 1089) but everything else is verb-flat. /api/tickets is GET-list / POST-create, but ticket-by-id has no GET handler. /api/templates is collection-style, /api/file-intent is action-style. Conventions drift per author.
tickets.proposed: Document an API style guide (use resource-style for entities, action-style for verbs) and reconcile over the next refactor pass.

tickets.title: main.js per-file HOOKS.FILE_INDEXED in a tight loop — no batching
tickets.summary: FILE_INDEXED fires synchronously inside the per-file await loop. If any subscriber registers I/O (Louis lock check, real-time UI) it serialises the indexer. No subscribers exist today but the hot loop is a tripwire.
tickets.proposed: Document the perf contract for FILE_INDEXED subscribers (must be < 5ms or registered async) OR change main.js to fire BULK_INDEXED with the file array after Pass 1.

tickets.title: terminal.js phone state machine has no external consumers beyond status line
tickets.summary: phoneOffHook is read only by app.js:103-111 (status line update). It suppresses signal pop-ups inside the terminal but is otherwise pure UI. The 'phone' metaphor is whimsical but undocumented for new contributors.
tickets.proposed: Either lean into it (route SSE notifications through the phone state machine globally — close the loop with the founder's signal/provisioned concept) or simplify to a 'mute notifications' toggle.
